artPrompt: A hushed nocturnal City Hall rotunda bathed in cold cyan moonlight, a single raised finger of a stern silhouetted clerk pressed to lips in extreme foreshortened close-up, demanding silence. Behind, dim corridors of filing cabinets recede into oppressive stillness, a blank smudged notice pinned to a bulletin board, faint arcane glow muffled and dim, an enforced quiet you can feel, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Quiet Hours Ordinance
text:
City Hall observes quiet hours. While it is after the loudest player at the table last laughed, opponents can't cast spells with the word "chaos," "goblin," "snack," or any exclamation point in their text.
Whenever an opponent raises their voice, cheers, or says "re-elected," you draw a card and issue a noise citation (they lose 1 life).
flavorText: A complaint was filed about the noise. The complaint was, regrettably, very loud.
